message:

+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current valkey setup",
+        "description": "Verify your valkey version and configuration:\n```bash\nvalkey-server --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working valkey installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review valkey configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant valkey configuration:\n**The problem/use-case that the feature addresses**\nMy customer has a use case where each element within a Hash/Set/Sorted Set data type would have different TTL. Right now, it is done by manually write client side logic or lua script."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support field level expire/TTL for hash, set and sorted set",
+        "description": "PTAL\n\nThis is a first draft of the robj hack.\n\nThere's a lot of duplicated code with createEmbeddedStringObject, but it's a PoC.\n\nThe EMBSTR is compact, but other robj are created with an expire field set to -1 (meaning no expire) and an embedded key pointer set to NULL.\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support field level expire/TTL for hash, set and sorted set\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],

+      "summary": "PTAL\n\nThis is a first draft of the robj hack.\n\nThere's a lot of duplicated code with createEmbeddedStringObject, but it's a PoC.\n\nThe EMBSTR is compact, but other robj are created with an expire field set to -1 (meaning no expire) and an embedded key pointer set to NULL.",
